title: Preserve Agent Plugin Lifecycle Dependency Direction
forbids: module router bypass of module context, root router bypass of impl.router and module routers, service imports of concrete providers or controller implementation, module-port re-exports from the retired internal bucket, controller release/evidence mechanics outside resource bindings, and any peer service client reachable from the lifecycle projection
why: The service owns lifecycle semantics behind module-local router handlers and type-only resource protocols. The root composes module routers through impl.router, runtime construction crosses declared CLI binding and provider-projection roots, and the qualified projection reaches one service client.
remediate: Implement procedure handlers from the local module surface, compose module routers through impl.router, keep ports protocol/type-only, construct concrete providers only in declared CLI binding or provider-projection roots, keep release/evidence mechanics in resource providers, and consume runtime values only through the canonical service client or an exact bindings/* package export.
message: Lifecycle dependencies must flow from impl.router through module routers and local module context, then from the qualified controller projection through one service client and exact binding facades to type-only resource contracts.
